channel.title: VMware Cloud Foundation APIs on My Cloudy World
channel.description: Recent content in VMware Cloud Foundation APIs on My Cloudy World
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2019–2026, Gary Blake; all rights reserved.

item.title: VCF Installer APIs: Deploying VMware Cloud Foundation JSON Using VCF Installer
item.author: gblake@jadenetworks.co.uk (Gary Blake)

description.p: Once you have validated your JSON specification file, along with the infrastructure validation you are ready to commence the deployment process. Once again we pass the JSON specification file as an input to the API request.

li.p: Connect to a system that has access to the infrastructure and is capable of running CURL.

item.title: VCF Installer APIs: Download Binaries from Broadcom Depot
item.author: gblake@jadenetworks.co.uk (Gary Blake)

description.p: After you have successfully configured the VCF Installer with a valid connection to the Broadcom Depot and synchronized the manifest, you can begin downloading the binaries required to implement VMware Cloud Foundation. Assuming your are deploying all components you will need to download 16 components as follows:

item.title: VCF Installer APIs: Validating Your JSON Using VCF Installer
item.author: gblake@jadenetworks.co.uk (Gary Blake)

description.p: The first step to automating the deployment of a new VMware Cloud Foundation platform is the creation and validation of your JSON specification file. This JSON specification file describes the environment to be configured.

There are a few ways in which you can build your JSON specification file, probably the easiest is through the use of the Planning and Preparation Workbook and a PowerShell utility my friend and colleague
Ken Gould
created called
VCF.JSONGenerator
(for a full rundown of the tool see Ken's blog post
Introducing the VCF.JSONGenerator PowerShell Module for VMware Cloud Foundation
).

item.title: VCF Installer APIs: Connect VCF Installer to the Broadcom Depot
item.author: gblake@jadenetworks.co.uk (Gary Blake)

description.p: Before you can begin deploying VMware Cloud Foundation, you first configure the VCF Installer to connect to the Broadcom Depot. In order to do this you must first obtain your unique download token from the VCF Business Services Console, once you have your download token you can proceed with setting up the VCF Installer.

item.title: VMware Cloud Foundation 9.1 APIs: Obtaining Authentication Tokens
item.author: gblake@jadenetworks.co.uk (Gary Blake)

description.p: Interacting with the public APIs of a VMware Cloud Foundation platform enables organizations to build custom end-to-end automation for their needs. The first step in the process involves obtaining an authentication token. A VMware Cloud Foundation platform consists of a number of core end points, each requiring slightly different parameters to carry out the interaction.
